name: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life
subtitle: Mechanical Design | Spring Engineering — DIN 2088:2009, ASTM A228/A227/A313

inScope: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

enum.label: Music wire ASTM A228 — E=206 GPa, Su=2060/d^0.163 MPa
Hard-drawn MB ASTM A227 — E=196 GPa, Su=1510/d^0.201 MPa
Chrome-silicon SAE 9254 — E=203 GPa, Su=1960/d^0.108 MPa
Stainless 302/304 ASTM A313 — E=193 GPa, Su=1867/d^0.146 MPa

standards.name: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life
standards.description: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life — Mechanical Design, Spring Engineering

formulas.reference: SAE HS-795 §7.3 — inner surface correction: K_i = (4C²−C−1)/(4C(C−1))

formulas.expression: const SU_params = {A228:[2060,0.163], A227:[1510,0.201], '9254':[1960,0.108], A313:[1867,0.146]}; const p = SU_params[wire_material]; Su_mpa = p[0] / Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, p[1])   // [MPa] Material Ultimate Tensile Strength (S_u) Reference: ASTM material strength curves — SAE HS-795 Table 1; Su = A/d^m (d in mm)

formulas.reference: SAE HS-795 §7.6 — static allowable: σ_allow = 0.78·S_u for torsion springs (bending mode)

formulas.expression: const E_map = {A228:206000, A227:196000, '9254':203000, A313:193000}; E_mpa = E_map[wire_material]; R_T_Nm_deg = E_mpa * Math.PI * Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, 4) / (3667 * mean_coil_diameter_mm * num_active_coils); T_calc_Nm = R_T_Nm_deg * angular_deflection_deg   // [N·m/°, N·m] Angular Spring Rate (R_T) & Stiffness Check Reference: DIN 2088 — R_T = Ed⁴/(3667·D_m·N_a) in N·m/°; E in MPa, d,D_m in mm
(fatigue_cycles >= 1e7) ? 0.45 * Su_mpa : 0.45 * Su_mpa * Math.pow(fatigue_cycles / 1e7, -0.085); SF_f = Se_mpa / sigma_b_mpa
formulas.reference: SAE HS-795 §8 — fatigue allowable: S_e = 0.45·S_u at 10⁷ cycles

warnings.message: Fatigue safety factor < 1.2: spring fatigue failure likely before target life — increase wire diameter, reduce stress ratio, or apply shot peening (+20% endurance)
Static SF < 1.5: marginal — spring may take permanent set under peak torque; consider SF ≥ 2.0 for critical applications per SAE HS-795 §7.6
Bending stress > 90% of UTS: spring operating near material limit — significant risk of brittle fracture; redesign with larger wire or lower C
SS wire at high stress: austenitic stainless has lower fatigue limit than music wire — consider Elgiloy or 17-7PH for high-cycle SS applications
Deflection approaching coil binding: spring may bind before reaching full deflection — verify body length and free-length tolerance

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required
